summary.rationale: RWR achieves 86.1% accuracy with substantial Cohen's kappa (0.74) and DARS 10.64 (large-scale strong band). Algebraic/ODE are marginally over 73% with moderate kappa (0.56-0.58) due to composite-member single-KO tests producing near-WT perturbations (ratios 0.99-1.02) that the direction_threshold=0.05 filter classifies as 'unchanged'. RWR's graph-propagation captures small signals where Algebraic/ODE's bounded-inverse dilution does not. Accuracy falls in the 80-95% band per §Decision table, so refinement is the appropriate next step focusing on (a) composite_collapse fixes for BR_SYN (DWF4/DET2/CPD should use gm=0.0), and (b) the SL topology sign issue from iter-3 Perception Gate (5 tests: T110-T113, T115).

comparison.strengths: Deterministic fixed-point solver; interpretable equations; perfect on large-effect perturbations (OE, full-family KO, multi-gene KOs). 100% accuracy on overexpression, double/triple/quadruple/quintuple_knockout.
comparison.weaknesses: Bounded-inverse inhibition and geometric-mean activation heavily dilute small-magnitude perturbations. Composite-member single KOs (gm=0.99) produce ratios 0.99-1.02 that fall within direction_threshold=0.05 and get classified 'unchanged' regardless of actual biology. 23 tests failed convergence (mostly composite-member cases). Per-class F1 for 'unchanged' is 0.07 (very low), showing the model cannot distinguish true unchanged from barely-moved perturbations.

comparison.best_params: K=0.5, n=3 (Hill activation: f(x)=x^n*(K^n+1)/(K^n+x^n), inhibition: g(x)=(K^n+1)/(K^n+x^n))
comparison.strengths: Hill functions give steeper sigmoidal response than bounded-inverse, improving sensitivity for moderate-effect perturbations (T041/T042 BZR1/BES1 gain-of-function caught where others missed some). Better MCC (0.52) than Algebraic (0.46). Convergence rate improves to 90%.
comparison.weaknesses: Still dilutes composite-member single KOs similar to Algebraic. Several rescue/Trap-5 tests fail (T033, T087-T091). Some large-ratio instability (T033 ratio 500000 indicates numerical blow-up under K=0.5, n=3 when GA rescue is applied to GA-insensitive background).

comparison.strengths: Graph propagation captures small-signal composite-member KOs that Algebraic/ODE classify as 'unchanged'. Per-class F1 for 'increased'=0.89, 'decreased'=0.90 (both excellent). 100% convergence. Cohen's kappa 0.74 (substantial band) is by far the best. DARS 10.64 in large-scale strong band. Fewer failures (16 vs 30-31).
comparison.weaknesses: Per-class F1 for 'unchanged'=0.00 — RWR cannot represent 'no change' well because any signed-graph propagation produces at least epsilon-sized signal. Struggles with signaling-mutant rescue (T033, T087, T089-T091, T097) because it cannot block hormone propagation when receptor is absent. Sign-conflict failures T110-T115 are topology-driven, not RWR-specific.
